signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh directional call in a defensive macro regime.
ETH remains the cleanest long on the board because price and open interest are rising together while funding is still flat, which supports a continuation long without obvious crowding.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable trend continuation, so I would not force a fresh position.
VVV is still extended and messy, with negative funding but a short-covering character, so it is better treated as watch-only until it either resets or rejects cleanly.
TON is too strong to fade and too extended to chase, so it stays on the watchlist for a future peak rejection instead of a fresh entry.
FARTCOIN is up 12.36% and still trend-supported, which makes it one of the clearest crowded-upside fade candidates on the sheet.
STBL remains one of the cleanest fades because price is up 8.44% while predicted funding is accelerating toward +1.27 bps, a classic sign of crowding risk.
LIT is one of the better secondary longs because price and open interest are both expanding together while funding is still not stretched.
WLD has momentum, but at more than 7% up on the day it already looks too extended to initiate while still lacking a clear rejection.
FOGO is already in a liquidation flush and does not look early enough to short fresh here.
MET has bullish mechanics, but liquidity is too thin to trust for a serious fresh position.
DASH still looks more like a short-covering bounce than a clean continuation entry, so I would keep it watch-only.
SPX is an acceptable secondary squeeze-long because funding is still negative while price is only modestly higher, though liquidity and conviction are both clearly below ETH.
DYDX is too flat and too low-liquidity right now to justify a fresh directional bet.
HEMI still leans bearish because price is rising on a short-covering pattern while funding is already positive and predicted to get even more crowded.
MANTA has strong squeeze mechanics, but the liquidity is too poor to elevate it beyond watch-only for now.
REZ has very strong squeeze fuel, but the setup still looks unstable and too low-liquidity to trust for a serious fresh long.
USTC is already in a mild flush state and does not offer a clean early entry in either direction.
LAYER is not showing enough structural or liquidity quality yet to deserve a fresh trade.
GOAT does not have enough signal quality or liquidity to justify a fresh position here.
